title: COMPARISON - The Thief of Joy | HG AMITASANA DAS | GITA LIFE Live
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: A warm welcome to all of you for today's discussion.
Geeta Live session on the topic comparison, the thief of joy.
It's a very very important topic because invariably we see people lead their lives comparing themselves,
their family members with others.
In fact, many of the goals, aspirations which people have stem out of that comparison.
In other words, we are led by what we see in people around us, which is not a very good
way about how we go about leading our lives.
So we will have the discussion on this topic based on the teachings, the instructions,
which Lord Shri Krishna has shared with us in the Bhagavad Geeta.
Before we start this discussion, let us all pray, seek the blessings of Prabhupada, Lord
Chaitanya, Lord Shri Krishna and then get into this discussion.
So please join me in offering these prayers.
You can fold your hands if possible, you can close your eyes and let us together offer
these prayers and then with the blessings of the Lord, delve into this topic.
Okay.
Nama om Vishnupadaya, Krishna preshthaya bhuta le,

So today we will be dividing this discussion in four or five parts.
The first section we will talk about why do we compare ourselves with others.
What is the root cause of that comparison?
What drives us?
What drives us into this comparison business?
That is first part.
Second part, we will discuss about why there is disparity in this world.
Apparently, we see that somebody is more fortunate, more blessed.

In Hindi, Insaan ko apne dukh se itna dukh nahin hota, jitna ki doosron ke sukh se.
In other words, man is not tormented more because of his pains and miseries, he is tormented
much higher degree by the pleasures when he sees in other human beings.
Insaan ko apne dukh se itna dukh nahin hota, jitna ki doosron ke sukh se.
In contrast to that, a Vaishnava, a devotee of the Lord is para dukha dukhi.
When he sees others suffering or miserable, he feels miserable.
When he sees others happy, he feels good about it and in fact he aspires, he is working
towards sarve sukhino bhavantu, let everyone be happy.

act do not take charge of law of karma
What a person is getting is what is due to him and assuming that if a person is doing
something wrong to achieve that so called material comfort, so called material prosperity
you will have to pay for it, just a question of time and we don't try to so to say put
our fingers in and try to change that law, the law will act and let's not become envious,
somebody is becoming prosperous, okay, God bless him.
If he is doing something wrong as a matter of duty what needs to be done for the right
correction we do it, but we don't become envious, we don't get into comparison business and
say why he is becoming prosperous, why not me, why I am left behind, why he is prospering,
all that we should avoid.

Shweta Kothari.
This question is from her, how do I stop comparison with my mother-in-law, she is not in KSC and
I am trying to encourage her but her ego comes often, at times I lose my patience.
I briefly mentioned more so when it comes to speaking to family members, we will find
a challenge because they have seen us acting in ways which are not very exemplary and hence
when we give them higher lessons, lessons from Gita.
They will not take it very seriously.
So better if we can put in touch with some devotee, maybe just like we have this kind
of discourses, maybe we can inspire them to hear them, who knows there is a possibility
that they may accept that instruction.
This question is from Mahesh Gabrani, how to surpass the results of past life karmas?
It is very simple, when we engage in devotional service of the Lord, Shravanam, Kirtanam, Vishnu,
Smaranam, Padas, Kirtanam, Kirtanam, Kirtanam, Kirtanam, Kirtanam, Kirtanam, Kirtanam, Kirtanam,
Shravanam, Kirtanam, Vishnu, Smaranam, Padasevanam, chanting, hearing about the Lord, glorifying
the Lord, Vandanam, offering prayers to the Lord, all the sins which we have done in the
past unknowingly, unintentionally, at times even intentionally will be burned to ashes
provided, provided we do not intentionally commit them again.
Devotional service should not become some kind of an eraser which gives me a license
to commit sin.
then wipe it out by doing some kind of devotional process no if i repent for what i have done in the
past unknowingly unintentionally and take a commitment that i will not repeat it in future
such devotional activities can burn the karmic reactions of all my previous karmas
